name: Charles Schwab
description_short: Full-service brokerage with commission-free trading, ThinkorSwim platform, and comprehensive wealth management services.
description_long:
Charles Schwab is one of the largest and most trusted brokerages in the United States, now even larger after acquiring TD Ameritrade. The firm offers commission-free stock and ETF trading, $0.65/contract options, and access to the powerful ThinkorSwim platform that came with the TD Ameritrade acquisition.

Schwab excels as a one-stop shop for investors, offering brokerage accounts, IRAs, 401(k) rollovers, banking services, and financial advisory. The combination of zero-commission trading with ThinkorSwim's professional-grade tools makes it an exceptional value for both casual investors and active traders.

The firm's research offerings are among the best in the industry, with proprietary research, third-party reports from Morningstar and Credit Suisse, and a wide range of educational content for investors at all levels.
pros: Commission-free trading with no account minimums
Full banking and wealth management services
cons: Futures commissions higher than some competitors
Transition from TD Ameritrade created some friction
